article_title: Connecting inflammation with glutamate agonism in suicidality

aggregated_relations.subject: Inflammatory stimuli
evidence_sentences: Inflammatory stimuli, such as central nervous system (CNS) infections, induce the kynurenine pathway of tryptophan degradation (Figure 1) and greatly increase CSF levels of kynurenine and its metabolites (Heyes et al, 1992).

aggregated_relations.subject: Inflammatory stimuli
aggregated_relations.object: CSF levels of kynurenine and its metabolites
evidence_sentences: Inflammatory stimuli, such as central nervous system (CNS) infections, induce the kynurenine pathway of tryptophan degradation (Figure 1) and greatly increase CSF levels of kynurenine and its metabolites (Heyes et al, 1992).

aggregated_relations.subject: KYNA
evidence_sentences: By contrast, KYNA, besides blocking the cholinergic α7 nicotinic receptor, antagonizes the glycine site of the NMDA-receptor (Hilmas et al, 2001; Stone, 1993).

aggregated_relations.subject: KYNA
evidence_sentences: By contrast, KYNA, besides blocking the cholinergic α7 nicotinic receptor, antagonizes the glycine site of the NMDA-receptor (Hilmas et al, 2001; Stone, 1993).

aggregated_relations.subject: QUIN
aggregated_relations.object: receptors containing the NR1+NR2A and the NR1+NR2B subunits
evidence_sentences: QUIN is an N-methyl-D-aspartate (NMDA)-receptor agonist, activating receptors containing the NR1+NR2A and the NR1+NR2B subunits (de Carvalho et al, 1996; Stone, 1993).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Interestingly, the NMDA-receptor antagonist ketamine was recently shown to reduce suicidality in four small-scale clinical studies (DiazGranados et al, 2010b; Larkin and Beautrais, 2011; Price et al, 2009; Zarate et al, 2012).

aggregated_relations.subject: immunotherapy with cytokines
evidence_sentences: In support of a causal relationship between inflammation and psychiatric symptoms, several studies show that immunotherapy with cytokines in patients induces depressive symptoms approximately 1 month after the beginning of the medication (Raison et al, 2009; Wichers et al, 2005).

aggregated_relations.subject: cytokines

contexts.time: approximately 1 month after the beginning of the medication

evidence_sentences: several studies show that immunotherapy with cytokines in patients induces depressive symptoms approximately 1 month after the beginning of the medication.

In support of a causal relationship between inflammation and psychiatric symptoms, several studies show that immunotherapy with cytokines in patients induces depressive symptoms approximately 1 month after the beginning of the medication (Raison et al, 2009; Wichers et al, 2005).

aggregated_relations.subject: Inflammatory stimuli
evidence_sentences: Inflammatory stimuli, such as central nervous system (CNS) infections, induce the kynurenine pathway of tryptophan degradation (Figure 1) and greatly increase CSF levels of kynurenine and its metabolites (Heyes et al, 1992).

aggregated_relations.subject: Metabolism
evidence_sentences: Metabolism further along this pathway produces several neuroactive compounds, including quinolinic acid (QUIN) and kynurenic acid (KYNA), both of which affect glutamatergic neurotransmission.

aggregated_relations.subject: QUIN
evidence_sentences: QUIN is an N-methyl-D-aspartate (NMDA)-receptor agonist, activating receptors containing the NR1+NR2A and the NR1+NR2B subunits (de Carvalho et al, 1996; Stone, 1993).

aggregated_relations.subject: interferon-γ
relation_raw_variants: are considered the major inducers of
evidence_sentences: Pro-inflammatory cytokines, especially interferon−γ, are considered the major inducers of indoleamine-2,3-dioxygenase (IDO-1), one of the enzymes regulating the first step of the kynurenine pathway (Guillemin, 2012).

chunks_extracted.section: CSF Levels of QUIN and KYNA
QUIN levels 6 months after the suicide attempt (follow-up)

aggregated_relations.subject: inflammation
evidence_sentences: In line with previous studies showing that inflammation can trigger generation of QUIN (Achim et al, 1993; Heyes et al, 1995), there was a significant correlation between CSF levels of QUIN and the pro-inflammatory cytokine IL-6.

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Moreover, this finding provides a neurobiological rationale for the recent reports describing that ketamine, by blocking NMDA-receptor transmission, can alleviate symptoms of suicidality (DiazGranados et al, 2010b; Larkin and Beautrais, 2011; Price et al, 2009; Zarate et al, 2012).

aggregated_relations.subject: KYNA
evidence_sentences: KYNA, which is mainly synthesized in astrocytes, blocks NMDA receptors and has neuroprotective and anticonvulsive properties (Erhardt et al, 2009; Schwarcz et al, 2012).
KYNA, which is mainly synthesized in astrocytes, blocks NMDA receptors and has neuroprotective and anticonvulsive properties.

aggregated_relations.subject: QUIN
evidence_sentences: In addition to being a direct NMDA receptor agonist, QUIN increases neuronal glutamate release as well as decreases glutamate uptake and recycling by astrocytes (Guillemin, 2012).
In addition to being a direct NMDA receptor agonist, QUIN increases neuronal glutamate release as well as decreases glutamate uptake and recycling by astrocytes.

aggregated_relations.subject: QUIN
aggregated_relations.object: glutamate uptake and recycling by astrocytes
evidence_sentences: In addition to being a direct NMDA receptor agonist, QUIN increases neuronal glutamate release as well as decreases glutamate uptake and recycling by astrocytes (Guillemin, 2012).

aggregated_relations.subject: interferon-α
aggregated_relations.object: CSF levels of QUIN, KYNA and pro-inflammatory cytokines
evidence_sentences: Important studies showing a causal relation between inflammatory factors and depressive symptoms have previously been performed in patients with hepatitis C, and demonstrated that peripheral injection with interferon-α increased CSF levels of QUIN, KYNA and pro-inflammatory cytokines along with depressive symptoms (Raison et al, 2010; Raison et al, 2009).

aggregated_relations.subject: interferon-α
evidence_sentences: Important studies showing a causal relation between inflammatory factors and depressive symptoms have previously been performed in patients with hepatitis C, and demonstrated that peripheral injection with interferon-α increased CSF levels of QUIN, KYNA and pro-inflammatory cytokines along with depressive symptoms (Raison et al, 2010; Raison et al, 2009).
